scenarios.title: Our Town Council
themes: How We Share Our Voice in the Community

listening.receptive: Can identify the speaker's perspective and intentions in presentations on local issues, such as public work projects or environmental initiatives (e.g., 'The council announced plans to improve the water system to address shortages. What is the intended impact of this change?').
listening.interactive: Can interpret and discuss details from audiovisual presentations on community topics (e.g., 'According to the speaker, I believe she means that by discussing recycling, we can help people change their habits, which is important.').
learning_outcomes: Can identify the speaker's perspective and intentions in presentations on local issues, such as public work projects or environmental initiatives (e.g., 'The council announced plans to improve the water system to address shortages. What is the intended impact of this change?').

Can interpret and discuss details from audiovisual presentations on community topics (e.g., 'According to the speaker, I believe she means that by discussing recycling, we can help people change their habits, which is important.').
Can ask and answer questions about main ideas in video presentations (e.g., 'What did you think the main idea was?' 'I think it was about the structure of town management.').

reading.reading: Can take notes on town council meeting texts, recording key points on decisions and civic duties (e.g., 'The city mayor stated that the Town Council is responsible for two main tasks: waste collection and road maintenance.').
reading.phonemic_awareness: Can outline civic responsibilities presented in articles, preparing for class discussions (e.g., 'The importance of voting and community feedback is emphasized in the article.').
learning_outcomes: Can take notes on town council meeting texts, recording key points on decisions and civic duties (e.g., 'The city mayor stated that the Town Council is responsible for two main tasks: waste collection and road maintenance.').
Can summarize arguments in articles about local governance, focusing on community involvement (e.g., 'The article argues that citizens should attend town meetings to influence decisions.').
Can outline civic responsibilities presented in articles, preparing for class discussions (e.g., 'The importance of voting and community feedback is emphasized in the article.').

speaking.productive: Can present an analysis of civic responsibilities, prompting classmates to share their views on community engagement (e.g., 'My analysis shows civic responsibility improves community well-being. What do you think?').
speaking.interactive: Can engage in role-play discussions simulating town council meetings to present key points and respond to community suggestions (e.g., 'The new park renovations were approved based on citizen feedback. What other suggestions should be implemented?').
learning_outcomes: Can present an analysis of civic responsibilities, prompting classmates to share their views on community engagement (e.g., 'My analysis shows civic responsibility improves community well-being. What do you think?').

Can engage in role-play discussions simulating town council meetings to present key points and respond to community suggestions (e.g., 'The new park renovations were approved based on citizen feedback. What other suggestions should be implemented?').
Can summarize classmates' ideas during discussions on local governance, keeping the conversation focused (e.g., 'We all agree that voting is a key responsibility of citizens.').

writing.productive: Can compose essays on civic engagement, using examples from the Town Council scenario (e.g., 'Citizens must vote to have a say in community decisions.').
writing.interactive: Can participate in online discussions about civic responsibilities, posting comments about local issues (e.g., 'I believe citizens should attend town meetings to share their views.').
learning_outcomes: Can compose essays on civic engagement, using examples from the Town Council scenario (e.g., 'Citizens must vote to have a say in community decisions.').
Can participate in online discussions about civic responsibilities, posting comments about local issues (e.g., 'I believe citizens should attend town meetings to share their views.').
Can respond to classmates' posts in a discussion forum, adding perspectives on community involvement (e.g., 'In my opinion, volunteering improves our town.').

mediation.text: Can paraphrase articles about local government projects using simple language and relevant visuals (e.g., shows an infographic with funding details and project phases and says, 'The city council allocated funds to renovate the sports complex.').
mediation.communication: Can facilitate discussions on civic duties by encouraging classmates to express their opinions, using gestures to prompt responses (e.g., uses an open-hand gesture to invite ideas by saying, 'What do you think about participating in community projects?').
learning_outcomes: Can paraphrase articles about local government projects using simple language and relevant visuals (e.g., shows an infographic with funding details and project phases and says, 'The city council allocated funds to renovate the sports complex.').

Can facilitate discussions on civic duties by encouraging classmates to express their opinions, using gestures to prompt responses (e.g., uses an open-hand gesture to invite ideas by saying, 'What do you think about participating in community projects?').

Can clarify complex information from civic articles by breaking down details and using key visuals for better understanding (e.g., shows a flowchart of the voting process and says, 'The article means that citizens should vote to have a say in town matters.').

grammar: Passive voice (e.g., 'Decisions are made based on community feedback.')
Reported speech (e.g., 'The mayor said that new policies would be implemented soon.')
Modals 'can,' 'should,' 'must,' 'may,' 'might' (e.g., 'Citizens must vote to have a say in community decisions.')
pronunciation: Practice the pronunciation of the short vowel sound /ɪ/ in words spelled with 'i' (e.g., public, civic) and 'y' (e.g., pretty, community, sustainability). Practice the pronunciation of words with the /eə/ sound followed by 'r' (e.g., share, serve). Continue practicing word stress in multisyllabic words and compound terms, such as 'decision,' 'participation,' 'responsibility,' 'authority,' 'feedback,' and 'public service'.

pragmatic: Asking for opinions on civic responsibilities (e.g., 'What do you think are important duties for citizens?'). Sharing viewpoints on community involvement (e.g., 'Citizens should vote to make their voices heard.').
sociolinguistic: Using formal language in civic discussions (e.g., 'Thank you for sharing your thoughts on our community.'). Showing respect in public dialogue (e.g., 'Could you tell us more about your ideas?').

projects.name: Civic Responsibility Campaign
projects.overview: Students develop a civic responsibility campaign, using the passive voice to describe decision-making processes in local government.
projects.general_objective: Create a digital campaign promoting civic engagement while practicing passive voice structures.
specific_objectives: Apply passive voice to describe government processes
Research local civic issues and responsibilities
Practice persuasive writing for community engagement

projects.name: Town Council Podcast
projects.overview: Students create a podcast explaining the town council's functions, using modals to express civic responsibilities.
projects.general_objective: Produce an educational podcast about local government using modal verbs for obligations and recommendations.
specific_objectives: Use modals (should, must, can) for civic duties
Develop scriptwriting and audio recording skills
Practice pronunciation and intonation for broadcasting

projects.name: Town Council Simulation
projects.overview: Students simulate a town council meeting, using reported speech to share citizen opinions.
projects.general_objective: Participate in a simulated town council meeting practicing reported speech and formal discussion language.
specific_objectives: Apply reported speech to present citizen views
Practice formal meeting procedures and etiquette
Use appropriate register for civic discussions

scenarios.title: Our Changing Forests
themes: I Explored the Ecological History Of...

listening.receptive: Can follow the linear sequence of points on a short history of local ecology (e.g., 'You can see from the photo in 2020 there was more rainforest in the area than in 2025.').
listening.interactive: Can respond to questions in arguments or debates on forest change (e.g., 'The author makes a good argument about cutting down the forest for farming. It has a big impact on the environment.').
learning_outcomes: Can follow the linear sequence of points on a short history of local ecology (e.g., 'You can see from the photo in 2020 there was more rainforest in the area than in 2025.').
Can respond to questions in arguments or debates on forest change (e.g., 'The author makes a good argument about cutting down the forest for farming. It has a big impact on the environment.').
Can identify and discuss key points in a structured talk on ecological issues (e.g., 'The first part of the talk was about the water cycle of rainforests.').

reading.reading: Can summarize and evaluate arguments in articles about local governance and civic engagement, focusing on diverse viewpoints (e.g., 'This article highlights that participation in town meetings can lead to better street repairs, but others argue that online surveys could be more inclusive.').
reading.phonemic_awareness: Can extract main points from articles on forest health and share them in reviews, noting environmental improvements (e.g., 'Our community's recycling program has successfully reduced waste in local landfills.').
learning_outcomes: Can summarize and evaluate arguments in articles about local governance and civic engagement, focusing on diverse viewpoints (e.g., 'This article highlights that participation in town meetings can lead to better street repairs, but others argue that online surveys could be more inclusive.').

Can analyze authentic texts about environmental restoration activities, evaluating their impact on the environment (e.g., 'Planting trees along the river has improved air quality and reduced erosion.').

Can extract main points from articles on forest health and share them in reviews, noting environmental improvements (e.g., 'Our community's recycling program has successfully reduced waste in local landfills.').

speaking.productive: Can give a speech about deforestation and its impact on local ecology (e.g., 'Deforestation has affected biodiversity in our area.').
speaking.interactive: Can engage in role-play discussions about ecological changes in the local area and propose environmentally responsible actions (e.g., 'If people planted more trees, the forests would recover faster.').
learning_outcomes: Can give a speech about deforestation and its impact on local ecology (e.g., 'Deforestation has affected biodiversity in our area.').

Can engage in role-play discussions about ecological changes in the local area and propose environmentally responsible actions (e.g., 'If people planted more trees, the forests would recover faster.').

Can participate in negotiations about environmental changes and ask follow-up questions to encourage detailed answers (e.g., 'If we limited urban expansion, would local wildlife have more space to thrive?').

writing.productive: Can create informative articles explaining local ecological changes and their impact on the community (e.g., 'If the town plants more trees, the air quality will improve.').
writing.interactive: Can create informative articles about environmental conservation guidelines, listing actions to protect local forests (e.g., 'If we prevent illegal logging, the forest ecosystem will recover faster.').
learning_outcomes: Can create informative articles explaining local ecological changes and their impact on the community (e.g., 'If the town plants more trees, the air quality will improve.').

Can create informative articles about environmental conservation guidelines, listing actions to protect local forests (e.g., 'If we prevent illegal logging, the forest ecosystem will recover faster.').

Can work with peers to produce a story about ecological changes, incorporating community conservation efforts (e.g., 'Over the past few years, our community has implemented several conservation projects that have significantly improved the local ecosystems.').

mediation.text: Can interpret classmates' non-verbal cues, like nodding or frowning, in discussions about environmental issues and adjust responses accordingly (e.g., uses a hand gesture to invite contribution and says, 'I noticed you seemed interested; would you like to share your thoughts on recycling?').
mediation.communication: Can facilitate discussions on ecological topics by prompting classmates to contribute ideas about conservation, using visuals like a deforestation map to spark conversation (e.g., 'How can we reduce pollution in our area?').
learning_outcomes: Can interpret classmates' non-verbal cues, like nodding or frowning, in discussions about environmental issues and adjust responses accordingly (e.g., uses a hand gesture to invite contribution and says, 'I noticed you seemed interested; would you like to share your thoughts on recycling?').

Can facilitate discussions on ecological topics by prompting classmates to contribute ideas about conservation, using visuals like a deforestation map to spark conversation (e.g., 'How can we reduce pollution in our area?').

Can interpret reactions, such as looking puzzled, during discussions on deforestation, using simpler language and drawings to explain (e.g., points to a simple diagram of a forest and says, 'Deforestation means cutting down trees, which harms animals' habitats.').

grammar: Present perfect (e.g., 'Deforestation has significantly affected biodiversity in our area.')
First and second conditional (e.g., 'If we preserve our forests, we will help reduce deforestation.' 'If people planted more trees, forests would recover faster.')
Comparatives (e.g., 'Urban ecosystems have less biodiversity compared to rural or natural habitats.')
pronunciation: Pronunciation of the long vowel sound /aɪ/ in words spelled with 'i' (e.g., 'life,' 'bio,' and 'diversity') and 'y' (e.g., 'try' and 'type'). Nouns and adjectives ending in -ate often have the ending reduced to /ɪt/ (e.g., 'climate,' 'approximate,' and 'accurate'). Word stress in multisyllabic words and compound terms, such as 'dramatic,' 'ecological,' and 'plastic-free'.

pragmatic: Asking about environmental impact (e.g., 'How has deforestation affected our area?'). Discussing conservation ideas (e.g., 'If we plant more trees, we can improve air quality.').
sociolinguistic: Expressing environmental concerns politely (e.g., 'Protecting our forests benefits everyone.'). Using community-focused language (e.g., 'Together, we can make a difference in conservation.').

projects.name: Forest Conservation Report
projects.overview: Students create a report on local forest health, using the present perfect to discuss environmental changes and conditionals to predict future consequences.
projects.general_objective: Write a comprehensive research report on local ecology using present perfect and conditional structures.
specific_objectives: Use present perfect to describe environmental changes
Apply first and second conditionals for predictions
Conduct research on local forest ecosystems
Practice academic writing and citation skills

projects.name: Forest Health Infographic
projects.overview: Students design an infographic using comparatives to raise awareness about deforestation.
projects.general_objective: Create an impactful infographic comparing environmental data using comparative structures.
specific_objectives: Apply comparative and superlative forms accurately

projects.name: Environmental Documentary
projects.overview: Students create a documentary about local forests, using the present perfect and conditionals to discuss conservation.
projects.general_objective: Produce a documentary film about local environmental issues using complex grammatical structures.
specific_objectives: Combine present perfect and conditionals in narration
Develop video storytelling and editing skills

listening.receptive: Can infer and discuss opinions expressed in simple lectures on environmental topics (e.g., 'Visiting the Sendero Los Quetzales in Volcán Barú National Park helps people feel calmer and more relaxed due to the serene natural environment.').
listening.interactive: Can participate in group discussions on global topics by sharing opinions (e.g., 'I think stress affects people's mental health by making them feel overwhelmed. Practicing mindfulness could really help reduce stress and improve focus.').
learning_outcomes: Can infer and discuss opinions expressed in simple lectures on environmental topics (e.g., 'Visiting the Sendero Los Quetzales in Volcán Barú National Park helps people feel calmer and more relaxed due to the serene natural environment.').

Can participate in group discussions on global topics by sharing opinions (e.g., 'I think stress affects people's mental health by making them feel overwhelmed. Practicing mindfulness could really help reduce stress and improve focus.').

Can actively participate in group discussions on local and global topics related to managing stress and mindfulness by asking clarifying questions to understand better others' opinions (e.g., 'Why are more students practicing mindfulness these days?' 'People are practicing mindfulness to stay calm during stressful times.').

reading.reading: Can compare examples from two wellness articles, evaluating how different stress-management techniques impact mental health (e.g., 'Guided meditation may work better for beginners, while mindfulness is effective for daily stress relief.').
reading.phonemic_awareness: Can recognize examples that illustrate wellness practices in informational texts, using these to support personal wellness plans (e.g., 'Listening to calming music can help me focus during study sessions.').
learning_outcomes: Can compare examples from two wellness articles, evaluating how different stress-management techniques impact mental health (e.g., 'Guided meditation may work better for beginners, while mindfulness is effective for daily stress relief.').

Can understand the connections between the main points of an article on stress-relief methods and examples of their impact on mental health (e.g., 'Deep breathing exercises calm the body and mind, reducing anxiety.').

Can recognize examples that illustrate wellness practices in informational texts, using these to support personal wellness plans (e.g., 'Listening to calming music can help me focus during study sessions.').

speaking.productive: Can describe personal achievements related to managing stress (e.g., 'Practicing mindfulness has helped me stay calm during stressful times.').
speaking.interactive: Can engage in storytelling by sharing personal experiences in managing stress and describing techniques like mindfulness (e.g., 'Walking in the Metropolitan Natural Park is a peaceful way to manage stress for me.').
learning_outcomes: Can describe personal achievements related to managing stress (e.g., 'Practicing mindfulness has helped me stay calm during stressful times.').

Can engage in storytelling by sharing personal experiences in managing stress and describing techniques like mindfulness (e.g., 'Walking in the Metropolitan Natural Park is a peaceful way to manage stress for me.').
Can answer questions about personal stress management practices, using examples to illustrate their effectiveness (e.g., 'Listening to calming music helps me focus before exams.').

writing.productive: Can write a comparative review of two wellness strategies, integrating real-life examples from local surroundings or meditation apps (e.g., 'The daily meditation app I downloaded is great, but hiking in the rainforest provides a unique sensory experience.').
writing.interactive: Can edit classmates' essays on wellness practices, suggesting improvements and clarifying points (e.g., 'Consider explaining how breathing exercises reduce stress.').
learning_outcomes: Can write a comparative review of two wellness strategies, integrating real-life examples from local surroundings or meditation apps (e.g., 'The daily meditation app I downloaded is great, but hiking in the rainforest provides a unique sensory experience.').
Can edit classmates' essays on wellness practices, suggesting improvements and clarifying points (e.g., 'Consider explaining how breathing exercises reduce stress.').
Can revise essays by adding suggestions of stress management techniques (e.g., 'You should include how meditation helps you focus.').

mediation.text: Can resolve misunderstandings in group discussions about wellness practices by rephrasing statements and using gestures to guide focus (e.g., demonstrates a calming hand gesture and says, 'Mindfulness means focusing on the present moment to reduce stress.').
mediation.communication: Can assist in assigning roles in wellness projects, ensuring that each classmate understands their responsibilities through visuals or examples (e.g., holds up a visual of relaxation techniques, and asks, 'Would you like to cover relaxation techniques?').
learning_outcomes: Can resolve misunderstandings in group discussions about wellness practices by rephrasing statements and using gestures to guide focus (e.g., demonstrates a calming hand gesture and says, 'Mindfulness means focusing on the present moment to reduce stress.').

Can assist in assigning roles in wellness projects, ensuring that each classmate understands their responsibilities through visuals or examples (e.g., holds up a visual of relaxation techniques, and asks, 'Would you like to cover relaxation techniques?').

Can clarify group goals for wellness projects by using bilingual support and local examples (e.g., provides an infographic in both Spanish and English and explains, 'Our goal is to share ways to manage stress with the class, such as visiting Parque Natural Metropolitano for nature walks.').

grammar: Gerunds (e.g., 'Practicing mindfulness has helped many people improve their focus and reduce stress.')
Modals for advice (e.g., 'You should try mindfulness.')
Present continuous for trends (e.g., 'People are practicing mindfulness to stay calm during stressful times.')
pronunciation: Practice the pronunciation of the long vowel sound /u:/ in most often words spelled with 'oo' (e.g., 'soothing,' and 'smooth'). Practicing alternation of full vowels and reduced vowels in multisyllabic words (e.g., mindfulness /ˈmaɪnd.fʊl.nəs/, beneficial /ˌbɛnɪˈfɪʃəl/).

pragmatic: Giving advice on stress management (e.g., 'You should try spending 10 minutes meditating in a quiet spot, such as your backyard or a park bench at Parque Omar.'). Sharing relaxation techniques (e.g., 'Listening to music helps me relax.' 'When I attend Zumba classes at the community center, I feel more energized and focused afterward.').
sociolinguistic: Offering suggestions for stress management politely (e.g., 'You could try relaxation techniques like deep breathing.'). Sharing personal wellness strategies, using appropriate language for social interaction, including personal experiences, and respectful tone (e.g., 'In my experience, exercise helps with focus.').

projects.name: Wellness Blog
projects.overview: Students create a wellness blog discussing stress-relief techniques, using gerunds to describe activities.
projects.general_objective: Create an informative wellness blog using gerunds as subjects and objects.
specific_objectives: Use gerunds effectively as sentence subjects
Research and present stress management techniques
Practice blog writing and online publishing

projects.name: Stress Management Video Tutorial
projects.overview: Students create a video tutorial on stress management techniques, using the present continuous to discuss wellness trends.
projects.general_objective: Produce instructional videos about wellness using present continuous for current trends.
specific_objectives: Apply present continuous for ongoing trends
Demonstrate stress management techniques on video
Develop video production and editing skills

projects.name: Wellness App Prototype
projects.overview: Students design a wellness app prototype to help manage stress, using modals to give advice.
projects.general_objective: Design a functional app prototype for stress management using modal verbs for recommendations.
specific_objectives: Use modals (should, could, might) for advice

scenarios.title: Preserving Our Heritage
themes: Learning How We Take Care of Our Past
What Should We Preserve in the Future?

listening.receptive: Can summarize the main ideas in a lecture about a historical Panamanian figure (e.g., 'The speaker talked about Victoriano Lorenzo, a Panamanian hero who fought for independence. He was a symbol of resistance against foreign control.').
listening.interactive: Can participate in discussions on literature, sharing insights from a story (e.g., 'In the story, the Emberá villages are depicted as a vital part of Panama's heritage.').
learning_outcomes: Can summarize the main ideas in a lecture about a historical Panamanian figure (e.g., 'The speaker talked about Victoriano Lorenzo, a Panamanian hero who fought for independence. He was a symbol of resistance against foreign control.').
Can participate in discussions on literature, sharing insights from a story (e.g., 'In the story, the Emberá villages are depicted as a vital part of Panama's heritage.').

Can respond constructively to classmates' views on cultural heritage by sharing examples and asking questions to deepen the discussion (e.g., 'I agree that the Emberá weaving tradition is important. What do you think about how it's passed down to younger generations?').

reading.reading: Can recognize a writer's point of view in texts on heritage preservation and explain their reasoning with textual evidence (e.g., 'The writer argues that historical traditions are crucial for national identity because they strengthen community pride.').
reading.phonemic_awareness: Can summarize main points and viewpoints in articles about heritage, preparing for classroom presentations (e.g., 'The author argues that preserving Casco Viejo is essential for maintaining Panama's cultural legacy and historical identity.' 'Another viewpoint emphasizes the importance of protecting indigenous traditions, such as the craftsmanship of the Guna molas, to ensure that future generations value and celebrate their cultural roots.').
learning_outcomes: Can recognize a writer's point of view in texts on heritage preservation and explain their reasoning with textual evidence (e.g., 'The writer argues that historical traditions are crucial for national identity because they strengthen community pride.').
Can analyze articles on cultural heritage, identifying examples that highlight preservation efforts (e.g., 'Restoring Casco Viejo attracts tourists and promotes local culture.').

Can summarize main points and viewpoints in articles about heritage, preparing for classroom presentations (e.g., 'The author argues that preserving Casco Viejo is essential for maintaining Panama's cultural legacy and historical identity.' 'Another viewpoint emphasizes the importance of protecting indigenous traditions, such as the craftsmanship of the Guna molas, to ensure that future generations value and celebrate their cultural roots.').

speaking.productive: Can narrate a story about personal experiences with cultural traditions, illustrating their importance for heritage preservation (e.g., 'During Corpus Christi celebrations in La Villa de Los Santos, I watched traditional dances that showcased our folkloric heritage.').
speaking.interactive: Can engage in storytelling to describe the personal importance of heritage (e.g., 'I visited the Panama Canal, which fills me with pride because my grandfather helped with its construction.' 'I visited the Panama Canal, where my grandfather worked during its construction.').
learning_outcomes: Can narrate a story about personal experiences with cultural traditions, illustrating their importance for heritage preservation (e.g., 'During Corpus Christi celebrations in La Villa de Los Santos, I watched traditional dances that showcased our folkloric heritage.').

Can engage in storytelling to describe the personal importance of heritage (e.g., 'I visited the Panama Canal, which fills me with pride because my grandfather helped with its construction.' 'I visited the Panama Canal, where my grandfather worked during its construction.').

Can explain the cultural significance of Indigenous heritage by describing traditional practices (e.g., 'The Guna people's traditional use of molas, brightly colored hand-woven textiles, is an important expression of their cultural identity and history.').

writing.productive: Can write emails to peers about the importance of preserving cultural heritage, sharing personal perspectives (e.g., 'I have learned about the importance of preserving both architectural sites like Casco Viejo and traditional crafts like mola weaving.').
writing.interactive: Can participate in writing exchanges discussing the significance of Panama's historical landmarks (e.g., 'I believe Portobelo should be preserved because it's part of our history.').
learning_outcomes: Can write emails to peers about the importance of preserving cultural heritage, sharing personal perspectives (e.g., 'I have learned about the importance of preserving both architectural sites like Casco Viejo and traditional crafts like mola weaving.').
Can participate in writing exchanges discussing the significance of Panama's historical landmarks (e.g., 'I believe Portobelo should be preserved because it's part of our history.').
Can compose letters to local officials, expressing opinions on cultural preservation efforts (e.g., 'It's essential to protect our heritage sites for future generations.').

mediation.text: Can explain the importance of heritage sites to classmates, using simple language and a map or image of a heritage site to emphasize cultural value (e.g., shows a timeline of restoration efforts and explains, 'Casco Viejo is important because it represents Panama's colonial past and restoration efforts.').
mediation.communication: Can summarize instructions for a field trip to a historical site, ensuring classmates understand the objectives by using visual aids (e.g., shows a timeline of events and says, 'We'll learn about Panama's history and see how it connects to today.').
learning_outcomes: Can explain the importance of heritage sites to classmates, using simple language and a map or image of a heritage site to emphasize cultural value (e.g., shows a timeline of restoration efforts and explains, 'Casco Viejo is important because it represents Panama's colonial past and restoration efforts.').

Can summarize instructions for a field trip to a historical site, ensuring classmates understand the objectives by using visual aids (e.g., shows a timeline of events and says, 'We'll learn about Panama's history and see how it connects to today.').

Can clarify the purpose of cultural preservation by using bilingual explanations and visuals in peer discussions (e.g., 'Preserving our heritage sites helps us remember and share our history,' with a translated summary if needed).

grammar: Relative clauses like 'which' and 'where' (e.g., 'Casco Viejo, which is a UNESCO World Heritage Site, attracts tourists from around the world.' 'The museum where the history of the Canal Zone is explained provides a great learning experience.')
Past simple vs. present perfect (e.g., 'The Panama Canal opened in 1914.' 'The Panama Canal has attracted many tourists.')
Future simple for predictions (e.g., 'We will continue to preserve our cultural and historical heritage.')
pronunciation: Practice the pronunciation of the long vowel sound /eɪ/ in most words spelled with 'a' + CV and 'ai' or 'ay' (e.g., 'trade,' 'became,' 'great,' 'say'). Practice the alternation of full vowels and reduced vowels in multisyllabic words (e.g., 'important': /ɪmˈpɔːtənt/, 'heritage': /ˈhɛrɪtɪdʒ/).

pragmatic: Asking about cultural significance: 'Why is preserving historic landmarks like Casco Viejo or the Panama Viejo ruins important?' Describing historical sites: 'The Panama Canal opened in 1914, becoming a pivotal part of Panama's global identity and transforming global trade.'
sociolinguistic: Discussing heritage respectfully: 'I learned so much about the Emberá community when I visited their village, and it made me realize the importance of their traditions in preserving our national identity.' Showing interest in cultural diversity: 'Hearing about traditional festivals from the Ngäbe-Buglé region made me want to participate and understand their cultural roots.'

projects.name: Cultural Heritage Website
projects.overview: Students design a website about Panama's historical sites, using relative clauses to describe essential locations.
projects.general_objective: Create a multimedia website showcasing heritage sites using relative clauses.
specific_objectives: Use relative clauses (which, where, that) for description
Develop web design and navigation skills

projects.name: Heritage Virtual Exhibition
projects.overview: Students create a virtual exhibition showcasing Panama's cultural heritage, using the present perfect to describe ongoing preservation efforts.
projects.general_objective: Build a virtual museum exhibition using present perfect for ongoing actions.
specific_objectives: Apply present perfect for ongoing preservation efforts

projects.name: Heritage Preservation Documentary
projects.overview: Students create a documentary about the importance of preserving historical sites.
projects.general_objective: Produce a documentary film advocating for heritage preservation.

scenarios.title: Panama's Role in the World
themes: The Importance of Panama in Global Trade
How the Canal Impacts Panama's Future

listening.receptive: Can comprehend and evaluate information from a news broadcast on a local issue (e.g., 'After evaluating the news broadcast, it's clear that the proposed improvements to the Panama Canal's infrastructure are designed to ease congestion and enhance global trade efficiency.').
listening.interactive: Can role-play resolving a disagreement over environmental concerns vs. economic growth (e.g., 'What would you say if a classmate suggested prioritizing development over sustainability?').
learning_outcomes: Can comprehend and evaluate information from a news broadcast on a local issue (e.g., 'After evaluating the news broadcast, it's clear that the proposed improvements to the Panama Canal's infrastructure are designed to ease congestion and enhance global trade efficiency.').
Can role-play resolving a disagreement over environmental concerns vs. economic growth (e.g., 'What would you say if a classmate suggested prioritizing development over sustainability?').
Can use questions to confirm understanding of key ideas in media broadcasts (e.g., 'Why did the speaker mention increased funding for infrastructure improvements around the Panama Canal?').

reading.reading: Can evaluate bias in texts about the Canal expansion, identifying whether the text emphasizes economic gains but downplays the impact on biodiversity (e.g., 'The text mentions job creation, but omits the effects on mangrove ecosystems.').
reading.phonemic_awareness: Can identify examples that support or contradict the argument that the Canal's expansion has increased national revenue at the expense of water conservation (e.g., 'Some reports praise trade efficiency, while others warn about water shortages in Lake Gatun.').
learning_outcomes: Can evaluate bias in texts about the Canal expansion, identifying whether the text emphasizes economic gains but downplays the impact on biodiversity (e.g., 'The text mentions job creation, but omits the effects on mangrove ecosystems.').
Can understand relationships between trade benefits and environmental concerns in Canal texts (e.g., 'While the Canal boosts trade, it also affects local ecosystems.').

Can identify examples that support or contradict the argument that the Canal's expansion has increased national revenue at the expense of water conservation (e.g., 'Some reports praise trade efficiency, while others warn about water shortages in Lake Gatun.').

speaking.productive: Can present an analysis of the Panama Canal's impact during the 2023 drought to explain decisions (e.g., 'New regulations were imposed to limit the number of daily crossings to conserve water.').
speaking.interactive: Can conduct interviews on global trade topics, asking about the Canal's effects on trade (e.g., 'How has the Panama Canal impacted global trade?' 'If Panama had not built the Canal, global trade would have faced significant challenges in the early 20th century.').
learning_outcomes: Can present an analysis of the Panama Canal's impact during the 2023 drought to explain decisions (e.g., 'New regulations were imposed to limit the number of daily crossings to conserve water.').

Can conduct interviews on global trade topics, asking about the Canal's effects on trade (e.g., 'How has the Panama Canal impacted global trade?' 'If Panama had not built the Canal, global trade would have faced significant challenges in the early 20th century.').
Can summarize responses from interviews, focusing on the economic impact of the Canal (e.g., 'It was explained that without the Panama Canal, shipping would have been slower and more costly.').

writing.productive: Can write analytical reports on the Panama Canal's effects, comparing revenue statistics and environmental findings (e.g., 'Since the expansion, the Canal has increased annual profits by three billion dollars but has also reduced water levels in surrounding lakes.').
writing.interactive: Can collaborate on writing a story about Panama's global impact, discussing its role in trade (e.g., 'Panama has always played an important role in global trade, thanks to the Panama Canal. If Panama invested more in its infrastructure, it could become an even more powerful trade hub in the future.').
learning_outcomes: Can write analytical reports on the Panama Canal's effects, comparing revenue statistics and environmental findings (e.g., 'Since the expansion, the Canal has increased annual profits by three billion dollars but has also reduced water levels in surrounding lakes.').

Can collaborate on writing a story about Panama's global impact, discussing its role in trade (e.g., 'Panama has always played an important role in global trade, thanks to the Panama Canal. If Panama invested more in its infrastructure, it could become an even more powerful trade hub in the future.').
Can work with classmates to outline a report on Panama's trade role, discussing key aspects like economic growth (e.g., 'Let's include sections on shipping routes and local job creation.').

mediation.text: Can paraphrase articles on the Panama Canal's impact on international trade, using visuals like a map comparing shipping routes through the Canal vs. around Cape Horn (e.g., 'The map shows that using the Canal saves two weeks of travel time for ships.').
mediation.communication: Can help negotiate roles in group projects on global trade, using gestures and encouraging eye contact to keep focus (e.g., 'Would you like to focus on the economic or environmental aspects of the Canal?').
learning_outcomes: Can paraphrase articles on the Panama Canal's impact on international trade, using visuals like a map comparing shipping routes through the Canal vs. around Cape Horn (e.g., 'The map shows that using the Canal saves two weeks of travel time for ships.').

Can help negotiate roles in group projects on global trade, using gestures and encouraging eye contact to keep focus (e.g., 'Would you like to focus on the economic or environmental aspects of the Canal?').
Can clarify main ideas in trade-related texts for group work, using visuals like pie charts to illustrate key concepts (e.g., 'Our project will cover how the Canal influences trade and local jobs.').

grammar: Passive voice (e.g., 'The Canal has been managed by the Panama Canal Authority since 1999.')
Second and third conditionals (e.g., 'If Panama had not built the Canal, global trade would have faced significant challenges in the early 20th century.')
Present Perfect Continuous (e.g., 'The Canal has been improving global trade, but it has also been causing environmental concerns.')
pronunciation: Practice the pronunciation of the long vowel sound /oʊ/, which is spelled 'o' alone or 'oa' + consonant or 'ow' (e.g., 'global,' 'local,' 'boat,' and 'show'). Practice the alternation of full vowels and reduced vowels in multisyllabic words (e.g., 'environmental' /ɪnˌvaɪrənˈmɛntəl/ and 'impactful' /ɪmˈpæktfʊl/).

pragmatic: Discussing the canal's impact (e.g., 'How has the Panama Canal been impacting global trade over the past few decades?'). Expressing hypothetical ideas (e.g., 'If Panama had not built the Canal, global trade would have faced significant challenges in the early 20th century.').
sociolinguistic: Talking about Panama's impact on trade respectfully (e.g., 'The Canal supports global commerce.'). Using specific trade-related terms appropriately according to the social or academic context (e.g., 'The Panama Canal is vital for international shipping.').

projects.overview: Students design an infographic explaining the role of the Panama Canal in global trade, using the passive voice to describe operations.
projects.general_objective: Create an informative infographic about global trade using passive voice structures.
specific_objectives: Apply passive voice for formal descriptions
Research global trade statistics and routes

specific_objectives: Apply present perfect continuous for ongoing impacts

scenarios.title: Entrepreneurial Spirit
themes: How to Market a Business Idea

listening.receptive: Can interpret instructions on using a computer program to present an original innovation (e.g., explains the steps for creating a presentation, such as: 'selecting a template layout, organizing key features on each slide, and adding images of the invention').
listening.interactive: Can respond to classmates' questions during a debate on whether Panamanian businesses should rely on automated customer service (e.g., 'I think chatbots make communication more efficient, but they lack the human touch that customers expect.').
learning_outcomes: Can interpret instructions on using a computer program to present an original innovation (e.g., explains the steps for creating a presentation, such as: 'selecting a template layout, organizing key features on each slide, and adding images of the invention').

Can respond to classmates' questions during a debate on whether Panamanian businesses should rely on automated customer service (e.g., 'I think chatbots make communication more efficient, but they lack the human touch that customers expect.').
Can ask for clarification when debating complex topics, like technology's impact on privacy (e.g., 'What do you mean by 'data security'?').

reading.reading: Can comprehend detailed instructions on launching a business, identifying key steps in planning (e.g., 'The guide recommends conducting market research before launching a product.').
reading.phonemic_awareness: Can summarize steps in technical instructions, preparing an outline for classmates (e.g., 'First, brainstorm and create a solid business idea that meets a market need. Next, draft a detailed business plan outlining goals, target audience, and financial projections. Afterward, register the business with the corresponding government entities. Finally, secure financing from banks or investors.').
learning_outcomes: Can comprehend detailed instructions on launching a business, identifying key steps in planning (e.g., 'The guide recommends conducting market research before launching a product.').

Can evaluate bias and perspectives in articles about marketing strategies and use this information to respond to classmates' questions during a debate on how Panamanian entrepreneurs should promote their business ideas (e.g., 'One article has emphasized influencer marketing as a key strategy but has ignored its cost and limited reach in local markets. I think small businesses should also consider community-based strategies that build trust.')

Can summarize steps in technical instructions, preparing an outline for classmates (e.g., 'First, brainstorm and create a solid business idea that meets a market need. Next, draft a detailed business plan outlining goals, target audience, and financial projections. Afterward, register the business with the corresponding government entities. Finally, secure financing from banks or investors.').

speaking.productive: Can give a speech on innovations, providing examples to support ideas (e.g., 'Businesses have started using renewable energy to cut costs and attract eco-conscious customers.').
speaking.interactive: Can collaborate on planning a business idea to discuss potential outcomes (e.g., 'If we advertise on social media, we might reach more customers.').
learning_outcomes: Can give a speech on innovations, providing examples to support ideas (e.g., 'Businesses have started using renewable energy to cut costs and attract eco-conscious customers.').
Can collaborate on planning a business idea to discuss potential outcomes (e.g., 'If we advertise on social media, we might reach more customers.').
Can lead discussions to share plans for ideas and evaluate others' ideas (e.g., 'I think launching an app could enhance customer engagement.').

writing.productive: Can write reviews of business innovations and ideas, evaluating their benefits for efficiency (e.g., 'This app helps track sales, which can improve customer service.').
writing.interactive: Can collaborate on creating business guidelines identifying product marketing strategies (e.g., 'Our guidelines should suggest social media advertising to attract customers.').
learning_outcomes: Can write reviews of business innovations and ideas, evaluating their benefits for efficiency (e.g., 'This app helps track sales, which can improve customer service.').
Can collaborate on creating business guidelines identifying product marketing strategies (e.g., 'Our guidelines should suggest social media advertising to attract customers.').
Can develop a collaborative guide for planning a business, assigning sections to each team member (e.g., 'I'll be analyzing the market trends, and you will be preparing the social media campaigns.').

mediation.text: Can simplify explanations of business concepts for classmates using hand gestures or examples (e.g., 'Marketing is like making a movie trailer; it's designed to grab people's attention while showing a sample video ad.').
mediation.communication: Can resolve misunderstandings in group discussions on business plans by rephrasing ideas and using simple visual examples (e.g., points to survey questions as an example, and says, 'Market research means finding out what people want to buy.').
learning_outcomes: Can simplify explanations of business concepts for classmates using hand gestures or examples (e.g., 'Marketing is like making a movie trailer; it's designed to grab people's attention while showing a sample video ad.').

Can resolve misunderstandings in group discussions on business plans by rephrasing ideas and using simple visual examples (e.g., points to survey questions as an example, and says, 'Market research means finding out what people want to buy.').

Can clarify tasks in group projects on entrepreneurship by using a checklist to make sure everyone understands their part (e.g., 'We need to describe our product and explain why people will want it.').

grammar: First conditional (e.g., 'If we advertise on social media, we might reach more customers.')
Present perfect for innovations (e.g., 'Local shops have begun using social media to tell more people about their products.')
Future continuous (e.g., 'I'll be analyzing the market trends.')
pronunciation: Practice the pronunciation of the long vowel sound /ɔː/, which is usually spelled 'o' + consonant, 'al' + consonant, or 'au'/'aw' (e.g., 'cost,' 'talk,' 'launch,' and 'law'). Practice the alternation of full vowels and reduced vowels in multisyllabic words (e.g., 'renewable' /rɪˈnuːəbl/, 'successful' /səkˈsɛsfəl/).

pragmatic: Asking about business strategies (e.g., 'What strategies have increased customer engagement?'). Sharing ideas for innovation (e.g., 'If we use social media, we can reach a larger audience.').
sociolinguistic: Using professional language for business ideas (e.g., 'This strategy could attract more customers to our project.'). Showing respect for innovative suggestions (e.g., 'Your idea adds an interesting approach.').

listening.receptive: Can interpret and explain instructions on using a professional networking site (e.g., 'Some steps include choosing a platform, creating an account, and entering your experience, such as internships in tech hubs in Panama like Ciudad del Saber.').
listening.interactive: Can facilitate and lead a group discussion on career interests, guiding classmates to share their goals (e.g., 'What skills do you think are important for a career in technology?').
learning_outcomes: Can interpret and explain instructions on using a professional networking site (e.g., 'Some steps include choosing a platform, creating an account, and entering your experience, such as internships in tech hubs in Panama like Ciudad del Saber.').
Can facilitate and lead a group discussion on career interests, guiding classmates to share their goals (e.g., 'What skills do you think are important for a career in technology?').
Can interpret classmates' career-related feedback during discussions on job skills (e.g., 'How would you improve your teamwork skills?').

reading.reading: Can recognize a writer's viewpoint on AI in articles, noting positive or cautious perspectives (e.g., 'The author is optimistic about AI's contribution to financial automation, such as Panama's advancements in fintech, but warns about job displacement in customer service roles.').
reading.phonemic_awareness: Can summarize technical details for classmates, ensuring understanding of AI concepts (e.g., 'The software learns from data inputs to improve its predictions.').
learning_outcomes: Can recognize a writer's viewpoint on AI in articles, noting positive or cautious perspectives (e.g., 'The author is optimistic about AI's contribution to financial automation, such as Panama's advancements in fintech, but warns about job displacement in customer service roles.').
Can comprehend technical instructions for AI software, explaining steps to peers (e.g., 'The manual instructs users to set up an algorithm and test it with data.').
Can summarize technical details for classmates, ensuring understanding of AI concepts (e.g., 'The software learns from data inputs to improve its predictions.').

speaking.productive: Can give a simple presentation about the future impact of artificial intelligence by expressing hypothetical outcomes (e.g., 'If AI continues to evolve, it might change the job market significantly.').
speaking.interactive: Can participate in debates, responding to arguments and agreeing or disagreeing with respect and clarity (e.g., 'It is agreed that AI could create jobs, but new skills may also be required.').
learning_outcomes: Can give a simple presentation about the future impact of artificial intelligence by expressing hypothetical outcomes (e.g., 'If AI continues to evolve, it might change the job market significantly.').
Can participate in debates, responding to arguments and agreeing or disagreeing with respect and clarity (e.g., 'It is agreed that AI could create jobs, but new skills may also be required.').
Can conduct interviews about AI and technology, asking open questions (e.g., 'How do you think Panama's logistics and trade industries could change with AI integration in the future?').

writing.productive: Can develop step-by-step instructions for using AI tools in everyday life, using simple language for clarity (e.g., 'Open the app and select a task to get suggestions from the AI assistant.').
writing.interactive: Can edit and revise essays on AI, ensuring clarity in descriptions of technology (e.g., 'Explain how AI assists in daily tasks like organizing schedules.').
learning_outcomes: Can develop step-by-step instructions for using AI tools in everyday life, using simple language for clarity (e.g., 'Open the app and select a task to get suggestions from the AI assistant.').
Can edit and revise essays on AI, ensuring clarity in descriptions of technology (e.g., 'Explain how AI assists in daily tasks like organizing schedules.').
Can provide feedback on classmates' writing about AI, suggesting examples to clarify abstract ideas (e.g., 'Add examples of AI used in customer service.').

mediation.text: Can resolve misunderstandings about AI's impact on society by using simple terms and gestures to explain (e.g., uses hand motions to simulate learning and says, 'AI is a technology that is programmed to learn from data and perform tasks like humans.').
mediation.communication: Can help assign roles in group projects on AI, using visuals or quick sketches to illustrate roles based on classmates' interests (e.g., 'Would you like to focus on AI in education or in the workplace?' with icons representing each sector).
learning_outcomes: Can resolve misunderstandings about AI's impact on society by using simple terms and gestures to explain (e.g., uses hand motions to simulate learning and says, 'AI is a technology that is programmed to learn from data and perform tasks like humans.').

Can help assign roles in group projects on AI, using visuals or quick sketches to illustrate roles based on classmates' interests (e.g., 'Would you like to focus on AI in education or in the workplace?' with icons representing each sector).

Can clarify the group's goals for projects on AI's societal impacts, ensuring each role is understood with visual aids (e.g., 'Our project will discuss how AI could change jobs and daily life,' using a timeline of technology evolution for context).

grammar: Conditional forms (e.g., 'If AI continues to evolve, it might change the job market significantly.')
Future simple (e.g., 'AI will create new employment opportunities.')
Passive voice (e.g., 'AI systems are programmed to learn from data and perform tasks.')
pronunciation: Practice the pronunciation of the long vowel sound /ɑː/, which is usually spelled 'o' + consonant and, in some common words, 'a' (e.g., 'robot,' 'job,' 'adopt,' and 'start'). Practice the alternation of full vowels and reduced vowels in multisyllabic words (e.g., 'innovative' /ˈɪnəvətɪv/, 'technological' /ˌtɛknəˈlɒdʒɪkəl/).

pragmatic: Asking about AI's future impact (e.g., 'How will AI change the job market?'). Sharing predictions about technology (e.g., 'AI could improve efficiency in many fields.').
sociolinguistic: Asking about technology thoughtfully (e.g., 'How might AI change jobs in Panama?'). Discussing AI's societal role sensitively (e.g., 'AI brings new possibilities but also some challenges for the future.').

scenarios.title: International English
themes: How English Is Used in Workplaces Globally
What Communication Skills Are Important in the Workplace?

listening.receptive: Can follow key points in a structured talk about career planning (e.g., Explain the speaker's advice on gaining experience, for example, 'He said that we should gain more experience in our field by taking on diverse projects or internships.').
listening.interactive: Can facilitate discussions on career options, prompting classmates to share ideas on communication-related skills (e.g., 'Which industries in Panama require English fluency, and why?').
learning_outcomes: Can follow key points in a structured talk about career planning (e.g., Explain the speaker's advice on gaining experience, for example, 'He said that we should gain more experience in our field by taking on diverse projects or internships.').
Can facilitate discussions on career options, prompting classmates to share ideas on communication-related skills (e.g., 'Which industries in Panama require English fluency, and why?').
Can ask follow-up questions to encourage classmates to elaborate on career choices (e.g., 'Can you explain which skills can help you advance your career?').

reading.reading: Can take introductory notes on articles about English as a global language, recording main ideas (e.g., 'English connects people from different cultures in business').
reading.phonemic_awareness: Can summarize points from texts on English in the workplace, preparing for discussions on language learning (e.g., 'The text argues that English skills can enhance career opportunities.').
learning_outcomes: Can take introductory notes on articles about English as a global language, recording main ideas (e.g., 'English connects people from different cultures in business').

Can recognize a writer's viewpoint on the spread of English, noting arguments about its importance in international business (e.g., 'The article highlights that sectors like logistics and finance in Panama rely heavily on English for communication.').
Can summarize points from texts on English in the workplace, preparing for discussions on language learning (e.g., 'The text argues that English skills can enhance career opportunities.').

speaking.productive: Can share future career aspirations, explaining the role English will play (e.g., 'I want to work in diplomacy, so learning English is essential.').
speaking.interactive: Can lead discussions about the role of English in global workplaces, prompting classmates to share perspectives (e.g., 'Why do you think English is important in international business?').
learning_outcomes: Can share future career aspirations, explaining the role English will play (e.g., 'I want to work in diplomacy, so learning English is essential.').
Can lead discussions about the role of English in global workplaces, prompting classmates to share perspectives (e.g., 'Why do you think English is important in international business?').
Can clarify classmates' points in discussions on global English, ensuring understanding (e.g., 'So, you believe English helps people connect across cultures?').

writing.productive: Can create narratives imagining future careers, highlighting the importance of English in global settings (e.g., 'As a diplomat, I'll use English to communicate with people worldwide.').
writing.interactive: Can participate in writing exchanges about future career goals, describing how English will be beneficial and including relevant workplace contexts and detailed examples (e.g., 'Learning English will help me communicate in an international business career by allowing me to negotiate contracts, participate in meetings, and collaborate with international partners.').
learning_outcomes: Can create narratives imagining future careers, highlighting the importance of English in global settings (e.g., 'As a diplomat, I'll use English to communicate with people worldwide.').

Can participate in writing exchanges about future career goals, describing how English will be beneficial and including relevant workplace contexts and detailed examples (e.g., 'Learning English will help me communicate in an international business career by allowing me to negotiate contracts, participate in meetings, and collaborate with international partners.').
Can write letters to classmates describing aspirations, mentioning English as a key skill (e.g., 'I want to become a doctor and use English to read medical research from around the world.').

mediation.text: Can explain the concept of English as a global language to classmates, using relatable visuals like a world map to show countries where English is spoken (e.g., 'English has become an international language, widely used in business to help people communicate.').
mediation.communication: Can summarize instructions for an English language workshop, using gestures to highlight key session goals (e.g., points to icons of travel and work, and says, 'We'll practice English skills for work and travel situations.').
learning_outcomes: Can explain the concept of English as a global language to classmates, using relatable visuals like a world map to show countries where English is spoken (e.g., 'English has become an international language, widely used in business to help people communicate.').

Can summarize instructions for an English language workshop, using gestures to highlight key session goals (e.g., points to icons of travel and work, and says, 'We'll practice English skills for work and travel situations.').

Can clarify the importance of English in international communication, using bilingual support and visuals as needed to convey the message (e.g., shows symbols of different career fields and explains, 'Knowing English can help in global careers like business and diplomacy.').

grammar: Present perfect (e.g., 'English has become an international language, widely used in business to help people communicate.')
Modals for necessity 'must,' 'could,' 'should,' and 'can' (e.g., 'It could be a good idea to gain experience through internships.')
Indirect questions (e.g., 'Can you explain which skills can help you advance your career?')
pronunciation: Practice the pronunciation of the long vowel sound /ɛ/, which is usually spelled 'e' + consonant and sometimes 'ea' (e.g., 'relevant,' 'necessary,' with one exception: 'says,' 'said'). Practice the alternation of full vowels and reduced vowels in multisyllabic words (e.g., 'communication' /kəˌmjuːnɪˈkeɪʃən/, 'accessible' /əkˈsɛsɪbəl/).

pragmatic: Asking about English's global role (e.g., 'Why has English become such an important language for international business?'). Discussing communication skills (e.g., 'Practicing English helps in cross-cultural workplaces.').
sociolinguistic: Using formal language in workplace contexts (e.g., 'Effective communication skills help us succeed globally.'). Recognizing cross-cultural communication needs (e.g., 'It's important to adapt language for different audiences.').
